facts.context: Which of the following would most likely provide examples of mitotic cell divisions?
facts.truth: longitudinal section of a shoot tip
distractors: cross section of an anther (site of pollen production in a flower)

facts.context: In a hypothetical environment, fishes called pike-cichlids are visual predators of algae-eating fish (i.e., they locate their prey by sight). If a population of algae-eaters experiences predation pressure from pike-cichlids, which of the following should least likely be observed in the algae-eater population over the course of many generations?
facts.truth: Selection for larger female algae-eaters, bearing broods composed of more, and larger, young
distractors: Selection for drab coloration of the algae-eaters
Selection for nocturnal algae-eaters (active only at night)
Selection for algae-eaters that become sexually mature at smaller overall body sizes

facts.context: Which of the following statements best describes what a Barr body is and its significance?
facts.truth: It is an inactivated X chromosome and results in females with half their cells having one X inactivated and the other half of their cells having the other X inactivated.
distractors: It is an inactivated Y chromosome and results in a man being sterile.
It is an inactivated Y chromosome, and the person who has it appears female.
It is an inactivated X chromosome and results in females who are sterile.

facts.context: Male satin bowerbirds adorn structures that they build, called "bowers," with parrot feathers, flowers, and other bizarre ornaments in order to attract females. Females inspect the bowers and, if suitably impressed, allow males to mate with them. The evolution of this male behavior is due to
facts.truth: sexual selection.

facts.context: Which statement about variation is true?
facts.truth: All new alleles are the result of nucleotide variability.
distractors: All phenotypic variation is the result of genotypic variation.
All genetic variation produces phenotypic variation.
All nucleotide variability results in neutral variation.

facts.context: Catastrophism, meaning the regular occurrence of geological or meteorological disturbances (catastrophes), was Cuvier's attempt to explain the existence of
facts.truth: the fossil record.

facts.context: Although mutations occur at a regular and predictable rate, which of the following statements is the LEAST likely reason the frequency of mutation appears to be low?
facts.truth: The predictable rate of mutation results in ongoing variability in a gene pool.
distractors: Some mutations produce alleles that are recessive and may not be expressed.
Some undesirable phenotypic traits may be prevented from reproducing.
Some mutations cause such drastic phenotypic changes that they are removed from the gene pool.

facts.context: This biome contains plants whose roots cannot go deep due to the presence of a permafrost.
facts.truth: Taiga

facts.context: In modern terminology, diversity is understood to be a result of genetic variation. Sources of variation for evolution include all of the following except
facts.truth: mistakes in translation of structural genes.

facts.context: Within a few weeks of treatment with the drug 3TC, a patient's HIV population consists entirely of 3TC-resistant viruses. How can this result best be explained?
facts.truth: A few drug-resistant viruses were present at the start of treatment, and natural selection increased their frequency.
distractors: HIV can change its surface proteins and resist vaccines.
The patient must have become reinfected with 3TC-resistant viruses.
HIV began making drug-resistant versions of reverse transcriptase in response to the drug.

facts.context: The higher the proportion of loci that are "fixed" in a population, the lower is that population's
facts.truth: A and C only

facts.context: Chemical substances released by organisms that elicit a physiological or behavioral response in other members of the same species are known as
facts.truth: pheromones

facts.context: Which group is composed entirely of individuals who maintained that species are fixed (i.e., unchanging)?
facts.truth: Aristotle, Linnaeus, and Cuvier

facts.context: Which of the following pieces of evidence most strongly supports the common origin of all life on Earth?
facts.truth: All organisms use essentially the same genetic code.

facts.context: This process leads to the net production of two pyruvate, two ATP, and two NADH.
facts.truth: Glycolysis

facts.context: Photosynthesis requires
facts.truth: light, CO2, water

facts.context: Which of the following statements explains how a point mutation can have no negative impact in the protein formed from a sequence in DNA?
facts.truth: Several codons code for the same amino acid.
distractors: The first codon of a gene is always AUG, a leader sequence; a mutation in that sequence will have no effect on the protein produced.
DNA polymerase and DNA ligase carry out excision repair before transcription.
RNA processing will repair point mutations before mRNA leaves the nucleus.

facts.context: Which of the following statements correctly characterizes an autoimmune disease?
facts.truth: T cells attack healthy body cells.
distractors: An aneurysm is a weakness in the wall of a blood vessel that causes it to balloon out. The vessel can rupture and cause a hemorrhage.
Several factors cause hypertension: smoking, being overweight, lack of physical activity, and too much salt in the diet.
Alzheimer's disease is caused by the buildup of plaque within the brain, causing brain cells to die.

facts.context: Logically, which of these should cast the most doubt on the relationships depicted by an evolutionary tree?
facts.truth: Relationships between DNA sequences among the species did not match relationships between skeletal patterns.
distractors: None of the organisms depicted by the tree ate the same foods.
Some of the organisms depicted by the tree had lived in different habitats.
The skeletal remains of the organisms depicted by the tree were incomplete (i.e., some bones were missing).

facts.context: An animal that is harmless copies the appearance of an animal that is dangerous as a defense mechanism to make predators think twice about attacking.
facts.truth: Batesian mimicry

facts.context: In a hypothetical population's gene pool, an autosomal gene, which had previously been fixed, undergoes a mutation that introduces a new allele, one inherited according to incomplete dominance. Natural selection then causes stabilizing selection at this locus. Consequently, what should happen over the course of many generations?
facts.truth: Both A and C
distractors: The proportions of both types of homozygote should decrease.
The proportion of the population that is heterozygous at this locus should remain constant.
The population's average heterozygosity should increase.

facts.context: Which of the following statements is not correct about lipids?
facts.truth: The head of a phospholipid is hydrophobic, and the tails are hydrophilic.
distractors: Lipids consist of fatty acids and glycerol.
The molecules of a saturated fatty acid are packed so close together that they form a solid at room temperature.

facts.context: Of the following anatomical structures, which is homologous to the wing of a bird?
facts.truth: Flipper of a cetacean

facts.context: Some strains of viruses can change normal mammalian cells into cancer cells in vitro. This transformation of the mammalian cell is usually associated with the
facts.truth: incorporation of the viral genome into the mammalian cell's nuclear DNA
distractors: formation of a pilus between the mammalian cell and the virus
conversion of the host's genome into the viral DNA
release of spores into the mammalian cell

facts.context: Which of the following is the source of oxygen produced during photosynthesis?
facts.truth: H2O

facts.context: Which of the following is an example of a detritivore?
facts.truth: Fungus

facts.context: The end product of the alcohol fermentation pathway is the alcohol ethanol. A considerable amount of energy still remains in ethanol, yet ethanol is a waste product for the yeast cells that produce it. What, then, is the purpose of fermentation?
facts.truth: Fermentation replenishes the NAD+ necessary for glycolysis to continue.
distractors: Fermentation provides CO2 for cellular respiration.
Yeast cells excrete ethanol in order to provide a defense against invading bacteria.

facts.context: A cell is placed into a hypertonic environment and its cytoplasm shrivels up. This demonstrates the principle of
facts.truth: plasmolysis.

facts.context: Natural selection changes allele frequencies in populations because some ____ survive and reproduce more successfully than others.
facts.truth: individuals

facts.context: Which statement about natural selection is most correct?
facts.truth: Well-adapted individuals leave more offspring, and thus contribute more to the next generation's gene pool, than do poorly adapted individuals.
distractors: Adaptations beneficial in one habitat should generally be beneficial in all other habitats as well.
Different species that occupy the same habitat will adapt to that habitat by undergoing the same genetic changes.
Adaptations beneficial at one time should generally be beneficial during all other times as well.
